task_completion.description: Three tracks named 'Piano_Atmos', 'Left_Monologue', and 'Right_Interruption' are created in the session.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the track headers on the left side of the timeline or the mixer strips. You should clearly see three tracks with the exact names 'Piano_Atmos', 'Left_Monologue', and 'Right_Interruption'.

task_completion.description: Audio regions are placed at the correct timeline positions: Piano_Atmos at 0.0s, Left_Monologue at 2.0s, and Right_Interruption at 5.0s.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the timeline in the editor window. The audio waveform block for Piano_Atmos should align with the 0:00 mark, Left_Monologue should align with the 2-second mark, and Right_Interruption should align with the 5-second mark.

task_completion.description: Left_Monologue is panned 100% Left and Right_Interruption is panned 100% Right.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the pan controls in the mixer window or track headers. The pan indicator for Left_Monologue should be turned completely to the left (L), and the pan indicator for Right_Interruption should be turned completely to the right (R).

task_completion.description: Track gains are set to -12 dB for Piano_Atmos, -6 dB for Left_Monologue, and 0 dB for Right_Interruption.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the volume faders or numerical gain readouts in the mixer or track headers. Piano_Atmos should show -12.0, Left_Monologue should show -6.0, and Right_Interruption should show 0.0.

task_completion.description: A fade-in of at least 2.0 seconds is applied to the Piano_Atmos region.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Piano_Atmos audio region on the timeline. There should be a visible diagonal line or curve at the beginning of the region indicating a fade-in, and it should extend from 0.0s to at least the 2.0s mark on the timeline ruler.

task_completion.description: Markers named 'Scene Start', 'Monologue', and 'Interruption' are placed at 0.0s, 2.0s, and 5.0s respectively.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the marker/location ruler at the top of the timeline. There should be visible marker flags or text labels reading 'Scene Start' at 0:00, 'Monologue' at 0:02, and 'Interruption' at 0:05.

integrity.description: The agent used the Ardour GUI to configure the session rather than editing the session XML file via terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Ardour application window open with active interactions (mouse cursors, open menus, dragging regions), not just a terminal window editing the 'MyProject.ardour' file.

integrity.description: The agent imported actual audio files rather than creating empty tracks or MIDI regions.
integrity.visual_evidence: The regions in the timeline should display visible audio waveforms (peaks and valleys), not empty blocks or MIDI piano roll data.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Ardour DAW.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface shown must be the standard Ardour digital audio workstation interface with its characteristic timeline, mixer, and transport controls, not a text editor or mock-up.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Track names required: 'Piano_Atmos', 'Left_Monologue', 'Right_Interruption'. Timeline positions: Piano_Atmos at 0.0s, Left_Monologue at 2.0s, Right_Interruption at 5.0s. Panning: Left_Monologue is 100% Left (0.0 azimuth), Right_Interruption is 100% Right (1.0 azimuth), Piano_Atmos is Center (0.5 azimuth). Gains: Piano_Atmos at -12.0 dB, Left_Monologue at -6.0 dB, Right_Interruption at 0.0 dB. Fade-in: Piano_Atmos requires a fade-in of at least 2.0 seconds. Markers: 'Scene Start' at 0.0s, 'Monologue' at 2.0s, 'Interruption' at 5.0s. Pass threshold is 60 points.
